content:
## Code Review — SCRUM-11
### Verdict: CHANGES_NEEDED
### Issues Found

- [severity: warning] [correctness] Employee data has 16 rows but ticket AC says "at least 15" — this is acceptable, but the generated AC comment says "exactly 15 rows." The current count of 16 meets the "at least 15" requirement, so this is not blocking.

- [severity: warning] [correctness] Sticky column headers not implemented — the generated AC (comment 10179) specifies "Column headers remain visible when scrolling through table data (sticky headers)." The `<thead>` has no `sticky top-0` class. With only 16 rows and pagination at 10, this is low-impact but technically missing. — Add `className="sticky top-0"` to the `<thead>` element.

- [severity: warning] [correctness] Sorting does not maintain pagination state — the generated AC (comment 10180) specifies "Sorting maintains pagination state (stays on current page)." Currently, sorting does not reset the page, which is the desired behavior. However, if the user is on page 3 and sorts a column that changes the total result count (e.g., after filtering), they could end up on an empty page. No explicit `setCurrentPage(1)` on sort is a reasonable design choice, but should be documented.

- [severity: warning] [correctness] Pagination controls are always visible even when results fit on one page — the generated AC edge case says "Pagination hides when filtered results fit on one page." Currently, pagination always renders even with totalPages === 1. — Consider conditionally hiding pagination when `totalPages <= 1`.

- [severity: warning] [style] Row key uses `${employee.name}-${employee.hireDate}` which is not guaranteed unique if two employees share the same name and hire date — Use a proper unique identifier or include the index as a fallback: `${employee.name}-${employee.hireDate}-${index}`.

- [severity: critical] [correctness] `EmployeesPage` is a server component that imports a `"use client"` `DataTable` component. This is correct and valid in Next.js App Router. However, the page itself has no `"use client"` directive, meaning it renders as a server component — which is the correct pattern. No issue here. *(Retracted — this is correct.)*

- [severity: warning] [correctness] The `filterData` function only checks `typeof value === "string"` — it will not match on numeric fields. For the current Employee type all fields are strings, so this works, but it silently skips non-string values. — Add a comment or convert all values to strings before matching.

- [severity: warning] [accessibility] The sort keyboard handler supports Enter and Space but does not implement arrow key navigation between column headers — WCAG recommends arrow key navigation in grid/table header contexts. This is a minor accessibility gap. — Consider adding left/right arrow key support for header navigation.

- [severity: warning] [testing] No test verifies keyboard-triggered sorting — the test "column headers are keyboard focusable" only checks `tabIndex` existence but does not simulate `Enter` or `Space` keypress to confirm sorting works via keyboard. — Add a test that uses `userEvent.keyboard('{Enter}')` on a focused column header and verifies sort changes.

- [severity: warning] [testing] No test for the edge case of long text truncation behavior — the component uses `max-w-[200px] truncate` on name, role, and department cells. No test verifies that overflow is handled. — Add a test with an employee whose name is very long to confirm truncation class is applied.

- [severity: warning] [security] The `employees-page.test.tsx` file hardcodes the expected row count as `16` (line 33: `expect(table).toHaveTextContent("16 rows")`). This is brittle — if sample data changes, the test breaks. — Use `employees.length` instead of the magic number.

### Summary

The implementation is solid and covers the core requirements well: the data table renders 5 columns of employee data with 16 sample rows, sorting works with asc/desc toggling across all columns (including chronological date sorting), search filters across all columns case-insensitively, pagination shows 10 rows per page with prev/next/page-number controls, alternating row shading is implemented, and the component has good ARIA labeling including `aria-sort`, `aria-label`, `aria-current`, and keyboard focusability on headers. The code follows project conventions (arrow function components, kebab-case files, proper import ordering, TypeScript strict types). Test coverage is comprehensive with 4 test files covering utils, data validation, component behavior, and page rendering. No security issues were found — no hardcoded secrets, no injection vectors, no auth bypasses. The two areas that need attention are: (1) pagination should hide when results fit on one page (per generated AC edge case), and (2) the row key strategy should be made more robust. Neither issue is blocking, but both should be addressed before merging for production quality.

## QA Matrix — SCRUM-11

### Overall: PASS

### Acceptance Criteria

| # | Criterion | Status | Evidence |
|---|-----------|--------|----------|
| 1 | Table displays employee data with columns: name, role, department, status, hire date | PASS | Unit test `renders table with correct column headers` confirms all 5 column headers. E2E snapshot shows all columns rendered with correct data. |
| 2 | Table has at least 15 rows of sample employee data with realistic values | PASS | Unit test `contains at least 15 records` passes (16 employees). Data includes realistic names, roles, departments, statuses, and ISO dates. |
| 3 | Column headers are clickable and sort table data in ascending/descending order | PASS | Integration tests `clicking column header sorts data and shows direction indicator`, `clicking same header toggles between asc and desc`, `clicking a different column sorts by that column` all pass. Unit tests cover sorting by all columns (name, role, department, status, hireDate) in both directions. E2E confirmed Enter key on focused header toggles sort. |
| 4 | Table rows have alternating background colors for visual separation | PASS | Unit test `applies alternating row background styling` confirms even rows have `bg-white` and odd rows have `bg-gray-50`. |
| 5 | Pagination controls display at bottom of table with page numbers and next/prev buttons | PASS | Integration test `renders pagination controls with page numbers and prev/next` confirms Previous, Next, and page number buttons. E2E snapshot shows `navigation "Table pagination"` with Previous, Page 1, Page 2, Next buttons. |
| 6 | Pagination shows 10 rows per page by default | PASS | Unit test `shows only 10 rows per page` confirms 11 total rows (1 header + 10 data). `PAGE_SIZE = 10` constant in data-table.tsx. E2E snapshot shows exactly 10 data rows on page 1. |
| 7 | Search bar above table filters rows based on text match across all columns | PASS | Integration test `search input filters rows across all columns` confirms filtering by "Engineering". Unit tests cover filtering by name, role, department, status, and partial hireDate. E2E snapshot shows search input above the table. |
| 8 | Table is responsive and displays appropriately on mobile devices (horizontal scroll or stacked layout) | PASS | E2E test at 375x812 viewport shows table adapting with text wrapping in cells. `overflow-x-auto` wrapper in data-table.tsx enables horizontal scroll when content overflows. Responsive container with `px-4 sm:px-6 lg:px-8` in employees page. Screenshot saved to `.harness/screenshots/mobile-employees-page.png`. |
| 9 | All colors match the COLOURS frame from the Figma design | NOT_TESTED | figma_design_spec is null in the ticket. No Figma color tokens available for comparison. Implementation uses Tailwind defaults (gray-50, gray-100, gray-200, gray-700, blue-500). |
| 10 | All typography (fonts, sizes, weights) match the FONT frame from the Figma design | NOT_TESTED | figma_design_spec is null in the ticket. No Figma typography tokens available for comparison. Implementation uses Tailwind defaults (text-sm, font-semibold, text-3xl font-bold for heading). |
| 11 | Table maintains accessibility standards with proper ARIA labels and keyboard navigation | PASS | Unit tests confirm: `role="region"` with `aria-label="Employee data table"`, `role="table"` with `aria-label="Employee directory"`, `aria-sort` on all column headers, `aria-label="Search employees"` on input, `aria-label="Table pagination"` on nav, `tabIndex={0}` on headers, `aria-current="page"` on active page button. E2E confirmed Tab moves focus from search to column headers, Enter triggers sort while focused. |

### Edge Cases

| Case | Status | Notes |
|------|--------|-------|
| Search with no results should display appropriate empty state message | COVERED | Test `shows empty state message when search has no results` confirms "No employees found." message. Implemented via colspan row in tbody when pageData is empty. |
| Sorting on date columns should handle various date formats correctly | COVERED | Tests `sorts by hireDate ascending` and `sorts by hireDate descending` confirm correct date sorting. `sortData` uses `Date.parse()` with ISO regex check `/^\d{4}-\d{2}-\d{2}/` to detect date columns. All data uses consistent YYYY-MM-DD format. |
| Pagination on last page with fewer than 10 items should display correctly | COVERED | Test `handles last page with fewer than 10 items` confirms page 2 of 16 items shows 6 rows. Test `clicking a page number navigates to that page` confirms page 3 of 25 items shows 5 rows (6 total with header). |
| Very long employee names or department names should truncate with ellipsis | COVERED | `max-w-[200px] truncate` class applied to name, role, and department cells in data-table.tsx. Tailwind `truncate` applies `overflow: hidden; text-overflow: ellipsis; white-space: nowrap`. |
| Table should handle rapid clicking on sort headers without breaking state | COVERED | T

{
  "ticket_id": "SCRUM-11",
  "plan_version": 1,
  "units": [
    {
      "unit_id": 1,
      "title": "Sample employee data and utility functions",
      "description": "Create the Employee type definition, 15+ rows of realistic sample employee data (name, role, department, status, hire date), and pure utility functions for sorting, filtering (case-insensitive text search across all columns), and pagination logic. Sorting must support ascending/descending on all columns including date columns. Pagination defaults to 10 rows per page and resets to page 1 when search filter changes. Include edge case handling: empty search results, last page with fewer than 10 items, rapid sort toggling.",
      "affected_files": [
        "src/lib/employee-data.ts",
        "src/lib/table-utils.ts",
        "src/__tests__/employee-data.test.ts",
        "src/__tests__/table-utils.test.ts"
      ],
      "dependencies": [],
      "test_criteria": [
        "Employee data array contains at least 15 records with all required fields (name, role, department, status, hireDate)",
        "sortData sorts ascending and descending by every column including date columns",
        "filterData performs case-insensitive text search across all columns",
        "filterData returns empty array when no rows match",
        "paginateData returns correct slice for page 1 (items 0-9), page 2 (items 10-14 if 15 total), etc.",
        "paginateData handles last page with fewer than 10 items",
        "getTotalPages calculates correct page count for various data sizes"
      ]
    },
    {
      "unit_id": 2,
      "title": "DataTable UI component with sorting, search, and pagination",
      "description": "Build a client-side DataTable React component that renders a table with: clickable sortable column headers (with sort direction indicator), alternating row background colors, a search/filter input bar above the table, and pagination controls below (page numbers, prev/next buttons, current page indicator). The component accepts typed employee data and integrates the sort/filter/pagination utilities from Unit 1. Must be responsive with horizontal scroll on mobile viewports. Include proper ARIA labels and keyboard navigation support. Long text should truncate with ellipsis via CSS.",
      "affected_files": [
        "src/components/data-table.tsx",
        "src/__tests__/data-table.test.tsx"
      ],
      "dependencies": [1],
      "test_criteria": [
        "Renders table with correct column headers: Name, Role, Department, Status, Hire Date",
        "Renders at least 15 data rows across pages (10 on first page)",
        "Clicking a column header sorts data and shows sort direction indicator",
        "Clicking same header toggles between ascending and descending",
        "Odd and even rows have different background styling (alternating rows)",
        "Search input filters rows by text match across all columns",
        "Search with no results displays an empty state message",
        "Search is case-insensitive",
        "Pagination controls render with page numbers and prev/next buttons",
        "Clicking next/prev updates displayed rows",
        "Only 10 rows display per page by default",
        "Pagination resets to page 1 when search filter is applied",
        "Table has proper ARIA attributes (role, aria-sort on headers)"
      ]
    },
    {
      "unit_id": 3,
      "title": "Employees page route and integration",
      "description": "Create the Next.js page at app/employees/page.tsx that imports and renders the DataTable component with the sample employee data. Add responsive container styling, page title/heading, and Tailwind utility classes. Add a navigation link from the home page to /employees. Ensure the page works as a client component where needed for interactivity while keeping the page shell as a server component if possible.",
      "affected_files": [
        "src/app/employees/page.tsx",
        "src/app/page.tsx",
        "src/__tests__/employees-page.test.tsx"
      ],
      "dependencies": [2],
      "test_criteria": [
        "Page renders at /employees route",
        "Page displays a heading for the employees table",
        "DataTable component is rendered with employee data on the page",
        "Home page contains a link to /employees",
        "Page container is responsive with appropriate padding/margins"
      ]
    }
  ]
}
